label: Model Selection Theory
description: How to choose models that generalize rather than memorize
core_tension: Simpler models underfit; complex models overfit. The goal is the sweet spot.
one_liner: Every model selection question is really asking: where is the bias-variance sweet spot?

label: Ensemble Methods
description: Combining many weak models into one strong predictor
core_tension: Individual trees are noisy; aggregation stabilizes predictions. Bagging reduces variance; boosting reduces bias.
one_liner: Bagging = parallel independent trees. Boosting = sequential error-correcting trees.

label: Database Design
description: Translating business requirements into structured, efficient data storage
core_tension: Model reality accurately (ERD) while eliminating redundancy (normalization).
one_liner: ERD captures the business; normalization purifies the structure; keys enforce integrity.

label: Mathematical Optimization
description: Finding the best decision subject to constraints
core_tension: Real decisions are constrained — LP/IP find the optimal solution within those constraints.
one_liner: Define variables → write objective → specify constraints → solve and interpret.

label: Uncertainty Modeling
core_tension: Point estimates hide risk; distributions reveal it. Both probabilistic and simulation approaches needed.
one_liner: From EMV in decision trees to Monte Carlo distributions — always characterize the spread, not just the mean.

label: Agentic AI Systems
description: AI that acts autonomously in multi-step workflows
core_tension: More autonomy = more capability but more risk. HITL controls and monitoring are the safety net.
one_liner: ReAct loop → LangChain chains → LangGraph state machines → HITL + LangSmith governance.

label: Natural Language Processing
description: Teaching machines to understand and generate human language
core_tension: Words lack inherent numerical meaning — embeddings and attention mechanisms bridge this gap.
one_liner: Word2Vec: words as vectors. Transformers: words in context via attention.

label: Classification Methods
core_tension: Interpretability vs accuracy. Logistic regression is transparent; SVMs/NNs are more powerful but opaque.
one_liner: Logistic = interpretable linear. kNN = instance-based. SVM = margin-based. NN = representation-based.

label: Financial Analysis
description: Valuing businesses and assessing investment risk
core_tension: Models require assumptions; AI can accelerate analysis but requires critical evaluation.
one_liner: 3-statement model → DCF valuation → Monte Carlo risk → AI-augmented research.

label: Ethics and AI Governance
description: Frameworks for responsible decision-making and AI deployment
core_tension: Efficiency and accuracy can conflict with fairness and accountability. Both matter.
one_liner: Distinguish ethics (institutional) from morality (personal); use both to evaluate AI systems.
